stories.headline: Iran launches retaliatory strikes on Gulf states as Trump warns US bombing could get 'much worse'
stories.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they had struck US military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait in response to fresh American strikes. They also warned their responses would expand to other bases across the region if US attacks were repeated.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Trump overshadows NATO summit with renewed Iran strikes
stories.summary: Renewed US strikes on Iran overshadow the close of the NATO summit, where allies pledged record defence spending and support for Ukraine. Meanwhile, the EU faces migration challenges, OLAF uncovers a counterfeit condom network and the World Cup reaches the quarter-final stage.

stories.headline: US launches more strikes on Iran with blasts reported in south of country
stories.summary: Trump says the attacks are "retribution for yesterday's bombing of ships by Iran". Iran earlier promised strong retaliation.

stories.headline: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race in Maine
stories.summary: The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

stories.headline: US carries out second day of strikes against Iran
stories.summary: Donald Trump claims Iran ‘called’ to negotiate but casts doubt on the prospects of a deal

stories.headline: Graham Platner drops Senate bid after rape allegations
stories.summary: Maine Democrat blames ‘corporate media system’ and ‘political establishment’ for campaign’s demise

stories.headline: Graham Platner Suspended His Senate Campaign. Here’s What’s Next for Democrats.
stories.summary: The deadline to pick a new nominee is July 27 and candidates are already lining up. State party leaders said they would hold some form of nominating convention.

stories.headline: Democrats Grow Frustrated as Graham Platner Resists Dropping Out Quickly in Maine
stories.summary: Two days after a woman accused Graham Platner of rape, he had not given up his Senate nomination in Maine, even as state Democrats announced plans to hold a nominating convention if he steps down.

stories.headline: US Senate nominee Platner halts campaign after assault allegations
stories.summary: A string of high-profile Democrats have withdrawn support for Platner following sexual assault allegation.

stories.headline: Security Precaution Led Trump to Use Old Air Force One in Leaving Turkey
stories.summary: The Secret Service is said to have asked that the president not use the Qatari-donated jet when he left Ankara. The swap deepens questions about the retrofitting of the new plane.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait again after deadly US raids
stories.summary: US attacks kill at least one person in the city of Iranshahr and cause widespread power outages in Chabahar.

stories.headline: Australia Agrees to Sell Uranium to India During Modi Visit
stories.summary: Australia has agreed to sell uranium to India for power generation in a landmark deal signed between the two nations during a visit to Melbourne by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

stories.headline: Temasek CIO Rohit Sipahimalani Warns on US Capex Surge Risks
stories.summary: Temasek International CIO Rohit Sipahimalani discusses investment opportunities and warns that a surge in US capital spending could create risks for markets in a wide-ranging interview with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Second Person in a Week Killed by Federal Task Force in Memphis

stories.headline: Trump’s Mixed Messages at NATO Summit

stories.headline: ‘Double cross, first class’: Why India’s Modi wins so many foreign awards
stories.summary: The prime minister was presented with an award during a visit to the Seychelles, adding to his large trophy collection.

stories.headline: IMF cuts 2026 world growth forecast, citing Iran war fallout

Pakistan Seeks Urgent LNG Cargo as Hormuz Attacks Disrupt Supply
stories.summary: Pakistan is urgently seeking to purchase a shipment of liquefied natural gas after fresh hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz again disrupted supplies.

stories.headline: Jupiter Fund Ditches US Treasuries in Favor of European Bonds
stories.summary: Jupiter Asset Management has cut US Treasury holdings to zero in one of its main bond funds, buying European government notes and adding to its hefty emerging-markets position instead.

stories.headline: Modi Kicks Off Two-Day Australia Visit
stories.summary:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese pledge to deepen economic and defense ties during talks in Melbourne. The two leaders are expected to sign agreements on defense and energy.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Japan’s Five-Year Bond Sale Sees Decent Demand on Higher Yields
stories.summary: Demand at Japan’s five-year bond sale on Thursday was broadly in line with recent auctions, showing that elevated yields are attracting some buyers.

stories.headline: China Asks Bankers to Avoid Underwriting Short-Term LGFV Bonds
stories.summary: China is steering municipal borrowers away from issuing short-term bonds in the domestic market, according to people familiar with the matter, in its latest bid to curb fundraising by weaker issuers.

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
stories.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

stories.summary: State Bank of India has so far raised more than $1.5 billion from foreign-currency deposits under a special facility launched last month for overseas citizens, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: Apple Supplier Luxshare Drops After $3 Billion Hong Kong Debut
stories.summary: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. fell in Hong Kong trading debut on Thursday after raising HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion) in the city’s biggest listing this year so far.

stories.headline: Tanks vs. tractors? Can the EU keep providing a political shield to farmers? MEPs debate in The Ring
stories.summary: Tough talks on the EU’s next long-term budget, Ukraine’s push for EU accession and skyrocketing fertiliser costs are putting European farmers under the political spotlight once more. Can the EU continue to appease them as it shifts its focus to defence and global trade? MEPs debate on The Ring.

stories.headline: 6 potential replacements for Graham Platner if he drops out of Senate race

stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.
